design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore underwater levels, find objects to solve puzzles, use bubble-firing mechanic to trap and defeat enemies
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Environmental hazards (radiation, toxic waste, leaking oil) vs. puzzle-solving and enemy combat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as a suave, intelligent anthropomorphic mudskipper secret agent protecting the seas, with James Bond spy thriller aesthetic
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong thematic commitment to James Bond parody (character design, level naming, mermaid love interests) combined with object-collection puzzle gameplay rather than pure action platforming. Puzzle-first approach distinguishes it from contemporary platformers like Sonic.

progression.upgrade_system: Object collection (keys, sponges) unlocks progression within levels; bubble mechanic serves dual purpose as weapon and puzzle tool
progression.unlock_mechanism: Key-finding and item acquisition gates progression; 12 levels with James Bond movie-themed titles
replayability_hooks: Level variety through themed environments (oil tankers, underwater lairs)
Puzzle diversity requiring different object combinations

world_building_techniques: Level naming as parody references (License to Bubble, A View to a Spill, Leak and Let Die, From Three Mile Island with Love)
Character concept: mutated mudskipper as spy protagonist
Environmental storytelling through hazard types (radiation, toxic waste, oil spills)
NPC mermaids as double agents mimicking Bond film trope
Supervillain Doctor Maybe as parody of Dr. No

danger_signals: Ocean radiation and toxic waste contamination
Leaking oil tankers threatening marine ecosystems
environmental_storytelling: Underwater setting with oil tanker hazards reflecting 1990 environmental concerns
Radiation and toxic waste as visible threats in level design
Rescue missions for captured marine life establishing player as protector
